stem: A factory produces standard and deluxe items. Each standard item requires 2 hours of assembly and 1 hour of finishing. Each deluxe item requires 3 hours of assembly and 2 hours of finishing. In one day, the assembly department worked 58 hours and the finishing department worked 34 hours. If the profit is $40 per standard item and $65 per deluxe item, what was the total profit, in dollars, for the day?
explanation: Let s represent the number of standard items and d represent the number of deluxe items. From the assembly department: 2s + 3d = 58. From the finishing department: s + 2d = 34. From the second equation, s = 34 - 2d. Substituting into the first equation: 2(34 - 2d) + 3d = 58, which gives 68 - 4d + 3d = 58, so -d = -10, meaning d = 10. Substituting back: s = 34 - 2(10) = 14. Total profit: 40(14) + 65(10) = 560 + 650 = $1,210.

distractorRationale.A: Results from swapping the profit values, computing 65(14) + 40(10) = 910 + 400 = 1,310, then making a subtraction error to get 1,090.
distractorRationale.C: Results from solving the system incorrectly by using 2s + 2d = 58 instead of 2s + 3d = 58, getting d = 12 and s = 10, then computing 40(10) + 65(12) = 400 + 780 = 1,180, and making an arithmetic error.
distractorRationale.D: Results from reversing s and d in the profit calculation: 40(10) + 65(14) = 400 + 910 = 1,310, then confusing with 1,420 due to further errors.
